euler_problems: Problem 23 - Non-Abundant Sums: A perfect number equals the sum of its proper divisors. A number is abundant if its proper divisors sum to more than itself. Find the sum of all positive integers which cannot be written as the sum of two abundant numbers. All integers greater than 28123 can be written as the sum of two abundant numbers.
explanation: The code contains a function that calculates and prints the sum of the proper divisors for each number starting from 12, and checks if it is abundant. An abundant number is defined as a number that is less than the sum of its proper divisors. However, the loop currently only checks the number 12 due to the incorrect loop condition, meaning the expected functionality to find multiple abundant numbers is not implemented.
name: 2010-05-01_projecteuler23
subject: projecteuler23
summary: This project identifies abundant numbers up to a specified limit using a C++ program.
